_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of information technology (IT) acquisition/procurement requirements.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of data subject rights management, including rights request intake, fulfillment workflows, response timelines, and documentation requirements.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI SP domain. SP-K-004 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: OP-K-008 (OP, cosine: 0.539), RC-K-007 (RC, cosine: 0.499).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.0/10 (raw 0.395). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge, requirements]. DCWF-only terms [acquisition, information, procurement, technology] vs WIDAI-only terms [data, documentation, fulfillment, intake, management, request] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.870 (P=0.874, R=0.867). Balanced P/R — the 7-word DCWF element and 17-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (99.8%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Knowledge of information technology (IT) acquisition/procurement requirements." and "Knowledge of data subject rights management, including rights request intake, fu". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.544 > STS 0.395 (gap +0.150). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [knowledge, requirements] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 14.3%. Shared tokens: [knowledge, requirements]. DCWF-only: [acquisition, information, procurement, technology]. WIDAI-only: [data, documentation, fulfillment, intake, management, request].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 99.8% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.
Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.150 suggests some vocabulary-driven inflation in candidate selection.
